items.title: Product reviews to word-count matrix
items.scenario: A startup has 10,000 labeled review strings. `CountVectorizer(max_features=5000, stop_words="english")` fit on training reviews builds a 5,000-column vocabulary. Review "great battery, great screen" becomes a sparse row with 2 in the "great" column, 1 in "battery", 1 in "screen"—zeros elsewhere.
items.explanation: Each unique token (after tokenization) maps to one column; counts encode how often terms appear. This matrix feeds classifiers like Multinomial NB or logistic regression.

items.title: TF–IDF down-weights boilerplate words
items.scenario: Legal documents all contain "agreement" and "party" in nearly every file, but "indemnify" appears only in liability clauses. `TfidfVectorizer` assigns low weight to "agreement" (high document frequency) and high weight to rare discriminative terms like "indemnify."
items.explanation: TF–IDF combines term frequency in a document with inverse document frequency across the corpus—emphasizing terms that distinguish documents rather than shared function words.

items.title: Streaming logs with HashingVectorizer
items.scenario: A security team classifies millions of daily log lines. `HashingVectorizer(n_features=2**18)` maps tokens into a fixed 262,144-dimensional hash space without storing a vocabulary—suitable when memory cannot hold every unique token.
items.explanation: Hash collisions trade a small amount of noise for bounded memory and stateless transform—no fit step learns a vocab, though you still must not tune on test labels.

items.title: Leaked vocabulary from refitting on all data
items.scenario: An engineer calls `vectorizer.fit_transform(all_texts)` including the hold-out test set before training. Rare test-only tokens enter the vocabulary; reported accuracy looks strong but production fails on genuinely unseen tokens.
items.explanation: Fit must use training data only inside CV or train split; otherwise idf and vocabulary statistics absorb test information—a classic extraction-stage leakage bug.
